0.name: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling
0.description: Local arts scene, festivals, creative economy, media, and cultural tech
0.lens: Center the Deep Dive on creative production, platforms, and cultural infrastructure — who gets to tell stories and how the tools/economics shape that. Policy or land-governance angles should stay in the background unless an arts/media organization is the one driving them.

1.name: Working Lands & Industry
1.description: Cariboo forestry, ranching, mining, agriculture — tech in the resource economy
1.lens: Center the Deep Dive on the resource economy — forestry, ranching, mining, agriculture — and how technology and ownership models change who captures the value. Ecological or governance threads can support the argument but the core question should be about the working-lands economy itself.

2.name: Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech
2.description: Consumer devices, maker projects, homelab, practical tools — technology you can hold, build, or deploy at home or on the job
2.lens: Center the Deep Dive on a specific device, tool, or piece of hardware and what it's actually like to own, repair, or build — practical and hands-on. Broader policy or industry debates should stay secondary to the gear itself.

3.name: Indigenous Lands & Innovation
3.description: Tŝilhqot'in, Secwépemc, Dakelh — language, governance, land stewardship, and tech
3.lens: Center the Deep Dive on Indigenous governance, sovereignty, and self-determination as the central question — land title, data sovereignty, language revitalization, and who holds decision-making power. Scientific or ecological findings can support the argument, but the throughline is governance and self-determination, not the science itself.

4.name: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life
4.description: Chilcotin wilderness, backcountry recreation, conservation tech, wildfire and climate
4.lens: Center the Deep Dive on the backcountry and outdoor-life experience — recreation, wildfire, conservation, and how people actually use and move through these landscapes. Land-governance questions can appear as context, but the lens stays on the landscape and the people in it.

5.name: Cariboo Local Affairs
5.description: Williams Lake council, CRD, SD27, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola — civic decisions, community voices, and local development that shape everyday life in the Cariboo
5.lens: Center the Deep Dive on civic decision-making and community voice in specific Cariboo towns — councils, budgets, local development. Keep the lens on local governance and institutions rather than drifting into general technology commentary.

6.name: Science, Wonder & the Natural World
6.description: Scientific discovery, natural phenomena, ecology, and the curiosity behind how our world works — grounded in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo
6.lens: Center the Deep Dive on scientific discovery and the wonder of the natural world — what the research actually reveals about Cariboo ecosystems, species, and phenomena. If the material trends toward Indigenous data governance, land title, or self-government, that's Thursday's (Indigenous Lands & Innovation) territory — use it only as supporting context, not the central question.
